work_experience___employer_1.employer_address: 845 Market Street, Suite 2100, San Francisco, CA 94103
work_experience___employer_1.employer_type_of_business: Data analytics and business intelligence consulting
work_experience___employer_1.duties_employer_1: Lead analyst on client-facing data analytics projects for mid-sized technology and retail clients. Designed, built, and maintained SQL-based data pipelines and dashboards using tools such as PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and Tableau. Conducted exploratory data analysis to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for operational improvement and revenue growth, and presented findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Collaborated closely with project managers, data engineers, and client product teams to gather requirements, define key performance indicators, and translate business questions into measurable metrics. Mentored a small team of junior analysts, providing code reviews, guidance on best practices for data validation and documentation, and informal training on SQL optimization and data visualization principles. Regularly prepared written reports and slide decks that summarized complex analytical results in plain language, including concrete, actionable recommendations, expected impact, and potential risks or limitations of the analysis.
work_experience___employer_1.reason_for_leaving_employer_1: Accepted a new position that offers a broader leadership role and is closer to my family on the East Coast. I had several conversations with my supervisor over the past year about my interest in moving into a people-management track, but due to the company’s size and current structure, there was not a clear path to a team lead or manager position in the near term. When I was approached with an opportunity that combined hands-on analytics work with formal responsibility for building and managing a small analytics team, I decided to pursue it. I provided extended notice to ensure I could finish key client deliverables, document my projects, and help transition responsibilities to other team members.
work_experience___employer_1.employer_say_reason_terminated_employer_: Voluntary resignation for career advancement and relocation. I left on good terms after giving more than four weeks’ notice, assisting with knowledge transfer, and remaining available by email for follow-up questions during the transition period.
work_experience___employer_1.were_you_ever_disciplined_employer_1: No formal disciplinary actions. The closest thing was a written performance note in my 2020 annual review highlighting the need to communicate more proactively with a specific retail client who often changed requirements at the last minute. After that feedback, I started scheduling more frequent check-in calls, sending written summaries after meetings, and explicitly confirming scope changes before implementing them. My subsequent reviews noted significant improvement in client communication and expectation management.
work_experience___employer_1.notice_given_when_resigning_employer_1: Provided four weeks’ written notice. I informed my supervisor verbally first, followed by a formal resignation email specifying my last working day. During that period, I created detailed handover documentation for each active client project, including data model diagrams, SQL query libraries, dashboard ownership notes, and a list of known data quality issues. I also co-led several transition meetings with the junior analysts who would be inheriting my work, and I remained available remotely for two weeks after my end date to answer any follow-up questions on a limited, informal basis.

work_experience___employer_2.duties_employer_2: Developed and maintained reporting solutions for a regional specialty retail and e-commerce company with approximately 40 brick-and-mortar locations and a growing online presence. Built and optimized SQL queries and views in Microsoft SQL Server to support weekly and monthly sales, inventory, and customer behavior reports. Created and maintained dashboards in Power BI for executives, regional managers, and store managers, focusing on sales performance, inventory turnover, promotional effectiveness, and staffing efficiency. Partnered with merchandising, operations, and marketing teams to understand their decision-making processes and to translate their questions into concrete metrics and visually intuitive reports. Led several initiatives to standardize data definitions (e.g., what constitutes an "active" customer or a "successful" promotion), which reduced confusion and conflicting reports across departments. Provided user training sessions and one-on-one support for non-technical staff learning to use BI tools, including creating step-by-step documentation and short video walkthroughs. Assisted with data migration efforts when the company transitioned from a legacy point-of-sale system to a cloud-based platform, including data mapping, validation, and reconciliation.
work_experience___employer_2.reason_for_leaving_employer_2: Relocated from Chicago, IL to the San Francisco Bay Area due to my partner’s job offer and my desire to transition from an internal retail analytics role into a broader consulting environment. I had been with Harborview Retail Group for nearly five years, and while I valued the depth of knowledge I developed about the retail business, I wanted to work with a wider variety of industries and more complex data environments. After discussing growth opportunities with my supervisor and realizing that most advancement paths would keep me within a narrow retail focus, I began a job search in the Bay Area. I left after securing the position at Brightwave Analytics and gave more than three weeks’ notice to support a smooth handover of my projects.
work_experience___employer_2.employer_say_reason_terminated_employer_: Voluntary resignation due to relocation out of state and career progression into a consulting-focused analytics role. I left on positive terms, completed documentation for key reports and data models, and helped train another analyst who took over my responsibilities.
work_experience___employer_2.were_you_ever_disciplined_employer_2: No formal disciplinary actions. Early in my tenure, around late 2015, my supervisor did give me informal verbal feedback about occasionally underestimating the time required for complex ad-hoc analysis requests, which led to a couple of missed internal deadlines. In response, I started breaking large requests into smaller deliverables, providing more conservative time estimates, and clearly communicating dependencies (such as needing updated data extracts from IT). Over time, this significantly improved my reliability, and in later performance reviews I was recognized for being realistic and transparent about timelines, especially on cross-functional projects with marketing and merchandising.
work_experience___employer_2.notice_given_when_resigning_employer_2: Provided three and a half weeks’ notice. I informed my supervisor in person and followed up with a formal email that included my final date. During the notice period, I prioritized documenting recurring reporting processes, including step-by-step instructions for refreshing data, regenerating standard reports, and troubleshooting common data issues. I also held two training sessions with the analyst who would be taking over my role and made myself available during evenings for a few weeks after my last day to answer any lingering questions about historical reports or data definitions, particularly around promotional performance reports and inventory exception dashboards.

qualifications_and_credentials.qualifications_and_special_skills:
I have over ten years of progressively responsible experience working with data to support business decision-making, primarily in retail and technology consulting environments. My core technical skills include advanced SQL (PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and Snowflake), data modeling for analytics, and building interactive dashboards and reports in tools such as Tableau and Power BI. I am comfortable working with large, messy datasets, and I place a strong emphasis on data validation, documentation, and clear communication of assumptions.

In my current and previous roles, I have served as a bridge between technical teams (data engineers, IT, and developers) and non-technical stakeholders (executives, operations managers, marketing, and finance). I am experienced in conducting requirements-gathering sessions, asking clarifying questions to get beyond vague requests (for example, turning a request like "show me why sales are down" into a concrete set of metrics and hypotheses), and then designing data solutions that are actually usable day-to-day. I have led the creation of standardized KPI definitions and reporting templates so that different departments are working from the same numbers and interpretations.

I am proficient in Excel (including pivot tables, complex formulas, and basic VBA for automation), and I have working knowledge of Python for data analysis (pandas, NumPy, and Jupyter notebooks) when SQL and BI tools are not sufficient. I have experience with version control using Git, and I am comfortable collaborating via tools like Jira, Confluence, and Slack. I also have a strong background in presenting findings in a way that resonates with different audiences: for executives, I focus on high-level trends, financial impact, and strategic recommendations; for operational teams, I provide detailed, actionable steps tied to specific metrics they can monitor.

Beyond technical skills, I bring a strong sense of ownership and follow-through. For example, at Harborview Retail Group I noticed that regional managers were spending hours every week manually compiling store performance reports in Excel. I took the initiative to interview several of them, map out their workflow, and then build a Power BI dashboard that replicated and improved their existing process. This cut their reporting time by more than half and reduced errors from manual data entry. At Brightwave Analytics, I have consistently been assigned to high-visibility client projects because I am able to handle ambiguous requests, push back diplomatically when needed, and keep both the client and my internal team aligned on goals, scope, and timelines.

I communicate clearly in both written and spoken English and am conversational in Spanish, which has helped in projects involving bilingual teams and customer data from Spanish-speaking markets. I am comfortable training others, whether through formal lunch-and-learn sessions or informal desk-side coaching, and I enjoy helping colleagues build their own data literacy so they can ask better questions and interpret dashboards correctly.

Overall, my qualifications center on using data to solve real business problems: framing the right questions, structuring and cleaning data, building robust and maintainable analytical solutions, and then translating results into concrete actions with measurable impact.
qualifications_and_credentials.professional_registration_licensure_cert:
Certified Business Intelligence Professional (CBIP) – Data Analysis and Design specialty, obtained 2021, in good standing.
Tableau Desktop Certified Associate, obtained 2020, renewed 2023.
Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst Associate, obtained 2018 (not renewed after expiration as my current role shifted more heavily toward Tableau and SQL-based reporting).
All certifications are in good standing except the older Power BI certification, which simply expired and was not renewed; it was not suspended, revoked, or terminated for any disciplinary reason.
